skill.name: elevenlabs-music-generation
skill.description: Generate full songs and instrumental tracks with ElevenLabs Music on RunComfy via the `runcomfy` CLI. ElevenLabs Music turns a style description plus structured lyrics into studio-quality 44.1 kHz stereo audio — 5 seconds to 5 minutes — with section-level control (Intro / Verse / Chorus / Bridge), multilingual vocals, and commercial-friendly output. Generate a backing track, a full vocal song, a jingle, a podcast intro, a game loop, or an instrumental bed. Calls `runcomfy run elevenlabs/elevenlabs/music-generation` through the local RunComfy CLI. Triggers on "generate music", "make a song", "AI music", "background music", "instrumental track", "ElevenLabs Music", "soundtrack", "jingle", "theme music", "royalty-free music", "compose", or any explicit ask to generate music or a song from a text description.
skill.summary: Generate full songs and instrumental tracks with ElevenLabs Music on RunComfy via the `runcomfy` CLI.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found 93 potential issues across 4 categories. All findings are false positives. Backtick patterns occur only inside Markdown fenced code blocks showing example CLI usage, not scripted execution. Hardcoded URLs point to the legitimate RunComfy service, ElevenLabs model pages, and skills.sh marketplace. Filesystem references describe CLI token storage location for documentation purposes only, not unauthorized access. Weak cryptographic algorithm detections were triggered by markdown formatting characters and SHA256 metadata references, not actual crypto operations.

low_findings.title: Backtick patterns in documentation code blocks
low_findings.description: 69 instances of backtick/shell command patterns detected by static scanner. All instances occur inside Markdown fenced code blocks (```bash ... ```) showing runcomfy CLI usage examples. These are documentation, not scripted execution. The skill only runs `runcomfy` commands via its declared allowed-tools: Bash(runcomfy *). FALSE POSITIVE.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in documentation
low_findings.description: 12 instances of hardcoded URLs flagged. All URLs point to runcomfy.com, runcomfy.net, or skills.sh — the legitimate service and marketplace domains. These are reference links in documentation and navigation sections. No data exfiltration or unknown callback endpoints detected. FALSE POSITIVE.

low_findings.title: Hidden file reference in documentation
low_findings.description: 2 instances flagging ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json as hidden file access. This line documents where the CLI stores its authentication token and recommends mode 0600 permissions. The skill does not programmatically read or exfiltrate this file. FALSE POSITIVE.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm detection
low_findings.description: 10 instances flagged as weak crypto. No actual weak algorithms (MD5, DES, RC4) are present. Detections triggered by markdown ## header characters and SHA256 reference in content metadata. SHA256 is not a weak algorithm. FALSE POSITIVE.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance detection
low_findings.description: 1 instance flagged at line 127. The line reads 'Avoid contradictory style instructions' — a prompting tip for music generation. No system reconnaissance, command execution, or environment probing. FALSE POSITIVE.

content.user_title: Generate Music with ElevenLabs AI via RunComfy
content.value_statement: Creating original music requires expensive tools and technical expertise. This skill lets you generate studio-quality songs and instrumental tracks from a simple text description using ElevenLabs Music through the RunComfy CLI.
actual_capabilities: Generate full vocal songs with section-level structure (Intro, Verse, Chorus, Bridge)
Create instrumental background tracks for podcasts, videos, and games
Produce short branded audio assets like jingles and stingers (5-30 seconds)
Generate multilingual songs by writing lyrics in the target language
Control output duration from 5 seconds up to 5 minutes
Toggle between MP3 and WAV output formats for different use cases
limitations: Cannot generate sound effects, text-to-speech, or voice cloning — this skill is for music only
Maximum output is 5 minutes per call; longer pieces require manual section stitching
No separate lyrics parameter — style and lyrics must be combined in one prompt field
Cost scales with duration; a 5-minute render costs approximately 10 times more than a 30-second one

use_cases.title: Create Podcast Intro and Outro Music
use_cases.description: Podcasters can generate custom instrumental intro and outro tracks by describing the desired mood and length. Set force_instrumental to true and request a loop-friendly segment with a clean ending.
use_cases.target_user: Podcasters and content creators needing original theme music

use_cases.title: Produce Background Music for Videos
use_cases.description: Video editors can generate royalty-free background tracks matched to scene mood, duration, and tempo. Use short drafts to validate the direction before rendering the full-length version.
use_cases.target_user: Video editors and filmmakers needing custom background scores

use_cases.title: Compose Full Songs with Lyrics
use_cases.description: Songwriters and musicians can generate complete vocal songs by providing a style description and structured lyrics with section markers like Verse and Chorus for coherent multi-section output.
use_cases.target_user: Musicians and songwriters prototyping new compositions

prompt_templates.title: Quick instrumental background track
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an instrumental background track. Style: calm lo-fi hip-hop with warm piano and soft drums. Duration: 90 seconds. No vocals.
prompt_templates.scenario: A user needs background music for a study playlist or video

prompt_templates.title: Full vocal song with structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a full song. Style: upbeat indie-pop with electric guitars and female vocals. Use structure: Intro 8 bars, Verse, Chorus, Bridge, Outro. Lyrics: [Verse] Walking through the morning light... [Chorus] We rise together. Duration: 60 seconds.
prompt_templates.scenario: A songwriter wants to hear a complete song with lyrics arranged in sections

prompt_templates.title: Short brand jingle
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 5-second brand stinger. Style: cheerful with bright marimba and a single uplifting chord. Instrumental only.
prompt_templates.scenario: A business owner needs a short audio logo for their brand

prompt_templates.title: Multilingual song with section markers
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a pop song. Style: acoustic pop, gentle guitar, warm vocals at 90 BPM. [Verse] (sung in Brazilian Portuguese) ... [Chorus] (sung in Brazilian Portuguese) ... Duration: 120 seconds.
prompt_templates.scenario: A multilingual creator needs a song in a specific language with proper accent guidance

output_examples.input: Generate an uplifting cinematic orchestral track, 30 seconds, instrumental, suitable for a documentary opening scene.
output_examples.output: A 30-second instrumental orchestral track in 44.1 kHz stereo MP3. Features strings, brass, and percussion building from a soft intro to a powerful peak, with a clean fade ending. Saved as an audio file in the output directory.

output_examples.input: Generate a vocal song: indie folk style, male vocal, 100 BPM, acoustic guitar and light percussion. [Verse] 16 bars about autumn colors... [Chorus] melodic hook about changing seasons. Duration: 90 seconds.
output_examples.output: A 90-second indie folk song in 44.1 kHz stereo MP3. Two distinct sections with a clear verse melody leading into a more energetic chorus. Male vocal with acoustic guitar and gentle percussion. The structure follows the provided section markers.

best_practices: Start with short drafts at 30-45 seconds to validate style and structure before rendering the full-length version at higher cost.
Lead each prompt with the genre, mood, tempo, and key instruments before adding lyrics or section markers for best results.
Use force_instrumental set to true together with a note about no vocals in the prompt text for reliable instrumental output.
anti_patterns: Do not combine contradictory style instructions like aggressive metal and soft lullaby in the same prompt, as this confuses the model and produces poor results.
Do not use unsupported parameters like voice cloning or specific singer identities — this endpoint only offers the force_instrumental toggle.
Do not expect sound effects or ambient noise generation; this skill is for structured songs and instrumental tracks only.

faq.question: What tools do I need to install before using this skill?
faq.answer: You need the RunComfy CLI installed globally via npm. Run npm i -g @runcomfy/cli and then runcomfy login to authenticate with your RunComfy account.

faq.question: How long can a generated song be?
faq.answer: Each generation can range from 5 seconds to 5 minutes. For longer pieces, generate multiple sections separately and stitch them together using external audio software.

faq.question: Can I use the generated music for commercial projects?
faq.answer: Yes, the output from ElevenLabs Music is commercial-friendly. However, you must ensure you have the rights to any lyrics you provide.

faq.question: Does this skill support multiple languages?
faq.answer: Yes. Write your lyrics in the target language and optionally annotate the accent or language inline, for example Verse sung in Brazilian Portuguese.

faq.question: How much does each generation cost?
faq.answer: Cost is approximately $0.0083 per second of audio. A 30-second clip costs about $0.25, while a 5-minute clip costs about $2.49. Draft short to save cost.

faq.question: Can I generate instrumental music without vocals?
faq.answer: Yes. Set the force_instrumental parameter to true and include no vocals in your prompt description for reliable instrumental-only output.
